commentary: Return-to-sport isn't a date on a calendar. It's a limb-symmetry number, a clean single-leg hop, and an athlete who trusts the knee again. Rush the timeline and you do the rehab twice.
Watched a kid drop two tenths off his 100 this spring by fixing one thing: his first three steps out of the blocks. Mechanics before mileage.
Five years building the sports-rehab program at Iron City Sports Medicine. Proud of where it is, hungry for where it's going.
Coaching high school track taught me more about patience than any clinical rotation. The work shows up months after you stop looking for it.
Graft choice matters. The rehab matters more. I'll take a disciplined athlete over a perfect surgery every time.
For recruiting parents: highlight reels open the door, transcripts keep it open. Both, early.
Patellar tendinopathy doesn't respond to rest. It responds to the right load, progressed honestly. Eccentrics, not excuses.
Birmingham kids deserve adults who show up. That's most of the job.
If your warmup looks the same in February as it does in May, you're leaving speed on the table.
Spent the morning breaking down sprint film frame by frame with an athlete. Nobody posts about this part. It's the part that moves marks.
Spent an hour on return-to-sport progressions with the DPT students this week. Teach the why, not just the protocol.
Off-season is where seasons are won. Base mileage now, PRs in the spring.
Studied at UAB, learned to coach on a high school track in Birmingham. The classroom gives you the why; the kids teach you the how.
5:15 AM, headlamp on, Crestwood South loop. The run nobody sees is the one that keeps the rest of the day honest.
To the parents asking about D1: a clean transcript and a coachable kid travel further than a viral 100m split. Recruiters notice who listens.
Hamstring strains aren't bad luck. They're a strength and exposure problem. Eccentric load in the off-season, sprint exposure year-round.
Sang with the men's choir at New Hope this morning, then graded a week of practice film tonight. Faith and the work run on the same fuel.
Proud of our crew at the Iron City Sports Medicine charity 5K. Every entry funded a brace or a session for a kid who couldn't cover it.
New year, same standard: show up early, load it right, progress it honestly. Resolutions fade; protocols don't.
Half marathon weekend in Birmingham. I'll be running the Mercedes-Benz half and cheering every athlete I've ever taped an ankle for.
Season opener tomorrow. Months of base, blocks, and film come down to who trusts their preparation. We're ready.
